articles.title: Nicolas Cage's Spider-Noir Gets a New Trailer—and It's Full of Villains!
articles.summary: A new trailer for Spider-Noir shows Nicolas Cage fighting classic Spider-Man bad guys like Sandman and a deep-cut villain called Megawatt. The show is set in the 1930s and follows Ben Reilly, a private investigator with spider powers. You can watch it in black-and-white or color when it comes out on May 25 on MGM Plus and May 27 on Prime Video.

articles.title: Tennis Star Carlos Alcaraz Will Miss the French Open Due to a Wrist Injury
articles.summary: Carlos Alcaraz, the two-time defending champion, has to skip the French Open because of a wrist injury. He hurt his right wrist at the Barcelona Open and tests showed he needs to rest. Alcaraz won the Australian Open this year and is one of the best players on clay. His rival Jannik Sinner said he hopes Alcaraz gets better soon.

articles.title: Miles Morales Actor Says a Venom Game Was Canceled—But Is That True?
articles.summary: Nadji Jeter, the voice of Miles Morales, said in an interview that a standalone Venom game and DLC were planned but canceled after actor Tony Todd passed away. However, a reporter named Jason Schreier says that's not true. Leaked documents from 2023 showed Insomniac was working on a Venom game, but so far no official announcement has been made.
